associatedMedia.headline: Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.
Kilchurn Castle, Loch Awe, Argyll, Scotland

Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

zh.Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿吉爾格勒諾爾
zh.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell於1390年出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾格勒諾爾。她於大約1410年與John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1458年在蘇格蘭阿吉爾享年68歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland: 苏格兰阿吉爾格勒諾爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell于1390年出生在苏格兰阿吉爾格勒諾爾。她于大约1410年与John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn在苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1458年在苏格兰阿吉爾享年68岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Gyllis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Late Latin. Danish and Swedish equivalent of Giles .

nameMeanings.name: "Egidia"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell was born in 1390, in Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland. She married John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn about 1410, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1458, in Argyll, Scotland, at the age of 68, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell est née en 1390, à Glenorchy, Argyll, Écosse. Elle a épousé John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn environ 1410, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1458, à Argyll, Écosse, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell wurde 1390 in Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland geboren. Sie hat um 1410 in Argyll, Scotland John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1458 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Argyll, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell è nata il nel 1390 nel Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland. Lei sposato John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn circa 1410, nel Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1458, nel Argyll, Scotland, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbellは1390年にスコットランドアルグイルグレノーチイで生まれた。約1410年にスコットランドアルグイルでJohn - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lornと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1458年にスコットランドアルグイルで68歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell(은)는 1390년 스컷랜드 아그이 글렌르c히에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1410년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1458년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell nasceu em 1390, em Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland. Ela casou-se com John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn aproximadamente 1410, em Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1458, em Argyll, Scotland, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell родилась в 1390 г. в местности гленорчи, аргилл, Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn около 1410 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1458 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell nació en 1390, en Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland. Se casó con John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn aproximadamente 1410, en Argyll, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1458, en Argyll, Scotland, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
